title: Afeyan, Nagler & Tu (2025): Spatial RCC analysis: RCTD Analysis
text:
Introduction
This is a book that contains all code to reproduce figures related to spatial analysis of TCR data associated with Afeyan, Nagler & Tu, et al (2025)
Note that while the code is identical, this book was rendered after final manuscript figures were generated and thus minor differences may arise, particularly for steps that are non-deterministic or rely on random number generators.

title: Afeyan, Nagler & Tu (2025): Spatial RCC analysis: RCTD Analysis
section: Tertiary lymphoid structures are reservoirs of stem-like tumor-specific T cells in renal cell carcinoma
text:
Tertiary lymphoid structures are reservoirs of stem-like tumor-specific T cells in renal cell carcinoma
Alexander B. Afeyan†, Adi Nagler†, Chloe R. Tu†, Amit Sud, Nourhan El Ahmar, Gabriel Roberti De Oliveira, Berkay Simsek, Mehdi Borji, Haley E. Sax, Cleo Forman, Sophia Liu, Patrick A. Ott, Toni K. Choueiri, Jennifer G. Abelin, Shuqiang Li, Kenneth J. Livak, Jeremy M. Simon, Derin B. Keskin, Fei Chen, Sabina Signoretti, Giacomo Oliveira‡ˆ, David A. Braun‡ˆ, Catherine J. Wu‡ˆ

Abstract
Tertiary lymphoid structures (TLS) are associated with improved response to immune checkpoint blockade across solid tumors1–3, but their impact on tumor-specific T cell responses remains incompletely defined. By tracking the spatial phenotype of T cell clones with confirmed anti-tumor specificity, we report the architecture of T cell responses in renal cell carcinoma (RCC). Across 24 RCC tumors, TLS-containing (TLS+) tumors were more infiltrated by exhausted CD8 T cells, but with a reduced terminal exhaustion transcriptional program compared to TLS- tumors. To investigate the effect of TLS localization on RCC-specific T cells, we screened 581 CD8+ and CD4+ T cell receptor (TCR) α/β pairs across six patients and identified 72 tumor-specific (TS) and 21 tumor-antigen-specific TCRs. Among these bona fide TS-TCR clonotypes, 45.8% of clonotypes infiltrated tumors, and 10.2% localized within TLS by spatial transcriptomics. TS-TCRs in TLS+ tumors preferentially colocalized with plasma cell transcripts (P<0.0001) compared to viral-reactive TCRs, and were associated with upregulated progenitor exhausted. However, in counterbalance, exhausted TCR clonotypes also colocalized with macrophages of inferred immunosuppressive phenotype concentrated within a myeloid boundary of the tumor (P=3x10-14). Our data reveal TLS as reservoirs of tumor-specific T cells with stem-like features that could be leveraged by T cell immunotherapies.

title: 2  Clean clusters on NeoVax + CancerCell data
text:
5.1 B cell
B_1, B_0 are good (B.Mem), B_2 contains contaminant cells (i think these are interacting cells with B cells), B_3 is good (Plasma), B_4 is probably cytotoxic T contaminant (although not catastrophic, this one is borderline), B_5 is contains too much erythroid markers, B_6 is myeloid contaminant, B8 seems like a fine plasma cell cluster to me, and B9 may contain tumor contaminant, as ALDOB is a PT marker.
Summary: B_0, B_1 (memory), B_3, B_8 (plasma), B_2, B_4, B_5, B_6, B_7, B_9 are unideal for LIANA because they contain markers of other genes in addition to clear B cell markers (one sees clearly that they do contain B cell transcripts too so at least the clustering algo makes sense)

unique(scrna$b_subcluster)

 [1] "NK"            "T"             "NORM.EPI"      "ENDO"         
 [5] "Myeloid"       "B_6"           "FIBRO"         "ERYTH"        
 [9] "NORM.PT"       "NORM.INTERCAL" "B_1"           "B_0"          
[13] "RCC"           "B_4"           "B_2"           "B_3"          
[17] "B_5"           "B_7"           "B_8"           "B_9"          

scrna@meta.data <- scrna@meta.data %>%
  mutate(b_labels = case_when(b_subcluster %in% c("B_0", "B_1") ~ "B.MEM",
                              b_subcluster %in% c("B_3", "B_8") ~ "B.PLASMA",
                              T ~ NA))

5.2 Myeloid
M_4 is DCs, M_2 is non-classical monocytes, M_1 is classical monocytes, M_14 is DCs, M_11 is M2-Macs, M_8, M_3, and M13 are T cell contaminants. M_0 is TAMs, M_9 is Mast cells, M_12 is pDCs, M6 is Macs, M_7 is M2-macs, M_10 is monocytes, M_15 is monocytes, M_5 is M2-mac.
Summary: M_0 and M_6 are Mac (these are likely anti-inflammatory as well, but not as much as M2s); M_5, M_7, M_11 are M2. Mac; M_1, M_10 are Classical Monocytes; M_2, M_15 are non-classical monocytes, M_4, M_14 is DCs; M_9 is Mast (don’t need for LIANA); M_12 is pDCs (not needed for LIANA); M_3, M_8, and M_13 should be discarded as contaminant

section: 12.1 Filter for source-target interactions of interest
text:
12.1 Filter for source-target interactions of interest

12.1.0.1 Alex’s code from GPT

liana_tls_pos$self <- ifelse(liana_tls_pos$source == liana_tls_pos$target, 1, 0)
liana_tls_neg$self <- ifelse(liana_tls_neg$source == liana_tls_neg$target, 1, 0)

#remove self-interactions (Tex-Tex, e.g.)
liana_tls_pos <- liana_tls_pos[liana_tls_pos$self == 0,] #down to 675168
liana_tls_neg <- liana_tls_neg[liana_tls_neg$self == 0,] #down to 666120

#isolate interactions of Tex cells and Tmem cells
liana_tls_pos <- liana_tls_pos[(liana_tls_pos$source %in% c("TEX", "TMEM", "TREG") | liana_tls_pos$target %in% c("TEX", "TMEM", "TREG")),] #285648
liana_tls_neg <- liana_tls_neg[(liana_tls_neg$source %in% c("TEX", "TMEM", "TREG") | liana_tls_neg$target %in% c("TEX", "TMEM", "TREG")),] #281820

#get rid of HLA interactions and CD3
liana_tls_pos <- liana_tls_pos %>% 
  filter(!str_detect(ligand.complex, 'HLA|CD8A|CD8B|^CD4$'))
liana_tls_pos <- liana_tls_pos %>% 
  filter(!str_detect(receptor.complex, 'CD3')) #278520

liana_tls_neg <- liana_tls_neg %>% 
  filter(!str_detect(ligand.complex, 'HLA|CD8A|CD8B|^CD4$'))
liana_tls_neg <- liana_tls_neg %>% 
  filter(!str_detect(receptor.complex, 'CD3')) #274758

hist(liana_tls_pos$aggregate_rank, breaks = 50)







hist(liana_tls_neg$aggregate_rank, breaks = 50)







#0.05 seems to basically cut off all non-sig interactions

liana_tls_pos <- liana_tls_pos %>% filter(aggregate_rank < 0.05) #5890 - 2%
liana_tls_neg <- liana_tls_neg %>% filter(aggregate_rank < 0.05) #6225 - 16%
